note: Header row. Real records start after first REFRESH. Schema: id, claim, supporting_findings, weight (0-1), verified (bool), verified_at, verifier, first_seen, last_updated, dossier, contradiction_count, context_decay_days, context_decay_recomputed_at. context_decay is days since verified_at, recomputed at run start. Claims older than 90 days without re-verification are downgraded to verified: false on next REFRESH.

claim: GPT-5.5 is the current OpenAI frontier model as of June 2, 2026, available with 1M context in Codex, $5/M input and $30/M output on the API, with Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7% and GDPval at 84.9%. Treated as High under OpenAI's Preparedness Framework for biology and cybersecurity. Generally available on AWS via Bedrock as of June 1, 2026.

The 2026 frontier across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google has shifted from raw intelligence gains to agentic capability — every major lab shipped agent-runtime primitives (Dynamic Workflows from Anthropic, Managed Agents + Dreaming + Multiagent Orchestration + Outcomes from Anthropic, Google Antigravity 2.0 and Gemini Spark from Google, deepagents v0.4/v0.5 from LangChain) on top of the model itself.

Anthropic's May 2026 Claude updates shipped two production-grade agent runtimes in one month: (1) Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code (May 28) — runs tens to hundreds of parallel subagents in a single session, with prompt cache preserved across mid-conversation System Messages; (2) Claude Managed Agents (May 12) — Dreaming for memory curation, Multiagent Orchestration with traceable subagent delegation, Outcomes (rubric-graded, +10pt task success on hardest problems), Webhooks, Self-hosted Sandboxes (Cloudflare/Daytona/Modal/Vercel) public beta, MCP tunnels research preview.

LangChain + LangGraph 1.0 (Oct 22, 2025) is the de facto standard for production multi-agent orchestration in 2026, with create_agent as the entry point, middleware as the customization surface, and LangGraph as the durable-execution runtime. Used by Uber, LinkedIn, Klarna, Rippling, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Cisco. 90M monthly downloads. 57% of orgs in State of Agent Engineering have agents in production, and LangGraph is the framework most often named in custom-agent builds.

Persistent agent memory is the active 2026 research frontier with no clear winner. Four distinct architectural approaches are live: Letta (MemGPT-derived, OS-inspired, memory blocks, programmable context, #1 model-agnostic OSS on Terminal-Bench), Mem0 (extractive facts → vector DB, 91% p95 latency reduction on LoCoMo), Zep (temporal knowledge graph), Cognee (knowledge graph over heterogeneous sources). arXiv surveys (Du 2026, 47-author Hu 2025) confirm the space is moving fast but no canonical solution. MemoryArena exposes a deep gap: models near-perfect on LoCoMo plummet to 40-60% on multi-session decision tasks.

Persistent agent memory introduces a novel security class that prompt-injection defenses do not cover. InjecMEM (Tian 2026) achieves 61.4% retrieval success + 76.6% conditional attack success; eTAMP (Zou 2026) shows environmental-observation poisoning that activates across future sessions; SpAIware (Herrador & Rehberger 2026) demonstrates indirect prompt injection via web content that plants persistent memory. Joint industry study (Nasr 2025) bypasses 12 recent prompt-injection defenses with >90% attack success.
Artemis II successfully launched on April 1, 2026 for a 10-day crewed lunar flyby mission, marking the first crewed mission beyond low Earth orbit since Apollo.

Artemis III was restructured in May 2026 from a lunar landing into a crewed Earth-orbit docking test with commercial HLS, targeted for late 2027; this pushed the first crewed lunar landing to Artemis IV in 2028.

SpaceX Starship Flight 7 (IFT-7) completed a full mission profile on May 27, 2026 — Super Heavy booster catch, Starship upper-stage orbital insertion, controlled reentry and soft splashdown. 11 of 14 HLS critical-path milestones qualified.

SpaceX Starship HLS ship-to-ship propellant transfer demo is now targeted Q4 2026 (slipped from Q3 2026 due to cryogenic coupling rework). This is the remaining critical-path item for Starship HLS qualification for Artemis III.

Blue Origin Blue Moon MK1 is on a SEPARATE critical path from Starship HLS; either provider can be selected for the Artemis III Earth-orbit docking test in late 2027, with the selection decision still pending. Blue Moon MK1 first orbital test flight scheduled NET Q2 2027.

markdown-it is the right markdown parser for v1: 100% Markdown 1.0, 100% CommonMark 0.31, full GFM, mature since 2014, plugin-based (markdown-it-anchor for heading IDs, markdown-it-container for Pandoc-style fenced divs, markdown-it-footnote, markdown-it-deflist), token-stream AST. Zero required dependencies. Used by Vue/VitePress/Astro as default. Trust 0.95. Build-time pre-render at file-save time; vanilla HTML output; CSS inlined in <head>; no client-side JS for the readable surface.

Pandoc is the right fallback for documents that need a full standalone HTML5 wrapper with metadata: --embed-resources produces a self-contained HTML file with no external dependencies via data: URIs, --standalone produces valid HTML with <head> and <body>, 40+ input formats including gfm, commonmark, commonmark_x. Cross-references markdown-it for the v1 default; Pandoc is the escape hatch for non-standard inputs (e.g., docx, rst).

CSS scroll-driven animations (W3C/CSSWG spec, MDN canonical, Chrome 115+ since Jul 2023, Safari 26, Firefox behind a flag) enables animation-timeline: scroll() and animation-timeline: view() patterns that run off the main thread. For v1, this is progressive enhancement only — not Baseline. The primary v1 animation path is vanilla-JS IntersectionObserver (~30 lines, Baseline since March 2019).

IntersectionObserver API is the primary v1 animation primitive: Baseline Widely available since March 2019, ~30 lines of vanilla JS, no framework, options include root / rootMargin / scrollMargin / threshold / delay / trackVisibility. Pattern: one observer per page, threshold 0.1, add CSS class 'is-visible' to target elements that fade in via CSS transition. Always honor prefers-reduced-motion: reduce.

Tufte-inspired long-form layout: cap heading depth at H3 (per Tufte's Feynman-lectures principle), sidenotes sparingly, ETBook-serif with system-font fallback, off-white #fffff8 + off-black #111111. text-wrap: pretty on body paragraphs (avoids orphans), text-wrap: balance on headings (equalizes line lengths). Cross-browser: Chrome 117+, WebKit/Safari 17.4+, Firefox 121+. Gwern.net's 4 design principles (aesthetically-pleasing minimalism, accessibility/progressive-enhancement, speed, semantic zoom) are the philosophical foundation; Tufte CSS is the implementation template.

Self-contained single-HTML-file is the right delivery shape: Pandoc --embed-resources pattern, no external requests, all content in initial raw HTML (HTTP Archive 2025 confirms AI crawlers don't render JS, so the self-contained pattern is also a content accessibility win). CSS inlined in <style> in <head>. JavaScript (if any) inlined at end of <body>. Target < 100KB total uncompressed for a 3,000-8,000 word dossier. v1: zero webfonts (system font stack).

Performance budget for v1: < 100KB total HTML uncompressed (~15-20KB gzipped, vs median web 2.86MB / 28x lighter). LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1, zero external requests, zero webfonts, zero third-party scripts. The fade-in animation MUST use opacity + transform: translateY() to avoid layout shift (CLS = 0). prefers-reduced-motion: reduce always honored. Defer to v2: field-measurement via web-vitals library.

The 'AI output landing page' is a real convergent pattern across major labs: Anthropic Claude Artifacts (Jun 21, 2024, GA Aug 28, 2024), OpenAI ChatGPT Canvas (Oct 2024), Perplexity Pages (May 30, 2024), Google Gemini Gems. All surface AI-generated long-form content in a dedicated, often side-paneled, often shareable, often interactive page. Mavis's vault HTML is the Mavis-archive instance of this pattern — the markdown dossier is the artifact, the HTML is the consumption surface. v1 is on the leading edge of an industry convergence.

Authoring convention for Mavis's markdown (so the render is clean): YAML frontmatter (title, author, date, tags, dossier_id), H1 once per page, H2 sections, H3 subsections (cap at 3 per Tufte), horizontal rule between major sections, code fences always with language, image alt text always, Pandoc-style fenced divs (::: callout / fade-in-stagger / collapse / source-trail) for render hints. Implemented via markdown-it-container plugin; portable to pandoc if Mavis later switches parsers.

content-deck-generator is the closest existing pattern in Mavis's catalog for v1: same input shape (markdown), same output shape (standalone self-contained HTML in a known location), same auto-refresh pattern (60s via meta refresh + inline countdown). Borrow the auto-refresh for the fleet-status surface; depart on theme (dossier surface should be light/auto by default, not forced dark) and on rendering features (add fade-in, Tufte-inspired layout).

The April 16, 2026 Federal Register notice 91 FR 20465 (Doc. 2026-07361, Docket No. FDA-2025-N-6895) is a MEETING notice for the PCAC July 23-24, 2026 meeting, not the Category 2 removal action itself. Signed by Grace R. Graham, Deputy Commissioner for Policy, Legislation, and International Affairs. Filed 4-15-26; 8:45 am. Pages 20465-20467.

PCAC will meet July 23, 2026, 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, and July 24, 2026, 8:00 a.m. to 3:50 p.m. ET, at FDA White Oak Campus, 10903 New Hampshire Ave., Bldg. 31 Conference Center, the Great Room (Rm. 1503), Silver Spring, MD 20993-0002. Public may also participate via online teleconferencing/video conferencing platform.

July 23, 2026 PCAC agenda: 4 peptides considered for inclusion on the 503A Bulks List — (1) BPC-157 (free base) / BPC-157 acetate [use evaluated: ulcerative colitis]; (2) KPV (free base) / KPV acetate [wound healing and inflammatory conditions]; (3) TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4, Fragment LKKTETQ) (free base) / TB-500 acetate [wound healing]; (4) MOTs-C (free base) / MOTs-C acetate [obesity and osteoporosis].

July 24, 2026 PCAC agenda: 3 peptides considered for inclusion on the 503A Bulks List — (1) Emideltide (also referred to as delta sleeping inducing peptide / DSIP) (free base) / Emideltide acetate [opioid withdrawal, chronic insomnia, and narcolepsy]; (2) Semax (free base) / Semax acetate [cerebral ischemia, migraine, and trigeminal neuralgia]; (3) Epitalon (free base) / Epitalon acetate [insomnia].

The 7 peptides on the July 23-24 PCAC agenda are being considered for INCLUSION on the 503A Bulks List (the legal compounding gateway under section 503A) — not for re-placement in Category 1, and not for any other interim-policy category. Federal Register notice text: 'the Committee will discuss the following bulk drug substances being considered for inclusion on the 503A Bulks List.'

Removal from Category 2 is NOT the same as inclusion on the 503A bulks list, and NOT the same as placement in Category 1. Per FDA's official 503A bulks hub page, FDA's interim enforcement discretion applies ONLY to Category 1 substances ('FDA does not intend to take action against a compounder for compounding drugs using bulk drug substances listed in category 1'). Category 2 substances are explicitly outside the interim policy's scope. Until FDA affirmatively places a substance on the 503A bulks list OR in Category 1 with enforcement discretion, compounding that substance carries regulatory risk.

12 peptide bulk drug substances will be removed from Category 2 within 7 calendar days of the April 15, 2026 notice (effective approximately April 22, 2026). Full enumeration: (1) BPC-157; (2) Cathelicidin LL-37; (3) Dihexa Acetate; (4) Emideltide (DSIP); (5) Epitalon; (6) GHK-Cu (for injectable routes of administration); (7) KPV; (8) Mechano Growth Factor, Pegylated (PEG-MGF); (9) Melanotan II; (10) MOTs-C; (11) Semax (heptapeptide); (12) Thymosin Beta-4, Fragment (LKKTETQ) (TB-500). Additionally, GHK-Cu (non-injectable) removed from Category 1. All 13 removals driven by nomination withdrawal, not by FDA safety reassessment. The 12-name enumeration is captured from two independent law-firm secondary sources (Frier Levitt, Orrick/JD Supra) that both attribute the list to the FDA's republished 503A Bulks List PDF.

A second PCAC meeting is scheduled before the end of February 2027 to consider the remaining 5 peptides: Cathelicidin LL-37, Dihexa Acetate, GHK-Cu (for injectable routes of administration), Mechano Growth Factor Pegylated (PEG-MGF), and Melanotan II. GHK-Cu (non-injectable, previously in Category 1) is also slated for this second meeting. Source attribution: secondary-corroborated across Frier Levitt and Orrick/JD Supra; primary PCAC meeting notice for the second meeting not yet published (will be required before end of February 2027).
